verses.text: David takes refuge in God, praying never to be ashamed, and asks the LORD to be his rock and fortress. He runs to God as his stronghold. The trusting soul finds in God a defence that will not fail.
David takes refuge in God, praying never to be ashamed, and asks the LORD to be his rock and fortress. He runs to God as his stronghold. The trusting soul finds in God a defence that will not fail.
David takes refuge in God, praying never to be ashamed, and asks the LORD to be his rock and fortress. He runs to God as his stronghold. The trusting soul finds in God a defence that will not fail.

He asks to be pulled from the hidden net and commits himself with the words, 'Into thine hand I commit my spirit.' These were Christ's own words on the cross. To place our soul in God's keeping is the safest rest in life and death.

He asks to be pulled from the hidden net and commits himself with the words, 'Into thine hand I commit my spirit.' These were Christ's own words on the cross. To place our soul in God's keeping is the safest rest in life and death.
He hates empty idols, trusts in the LORD, and rejoices in His mercy, set free in a 'large room.' Deliverance brings gladness. God leads His own out of straits into spacious freedom.
He hates empty idols, trusts in the LORD, and rejoices in His mercy, set free in a 'large room.' Deliverance brings gladness. God leads His own out of straits into spacious freedom.
He hates empty idols, trusts in the LORD, and rejoices in His mercy, set free in a 'large room.' Deliverance brings gladness. God leads His own out of straits into spacious freedom.
He pleads for mercy, for grief has consumed his eyes, soul, and strength. His sorrow is whole and deep. The believer may lay even his wasting and weakness before God.
He pleads for mercy, for grief has consumed his eyes, soul, and strength. His sorrow is whole and deep. The believer may lay even his wasting and weakness before God.

He is scorned by neighbours and dreaded by friends, 'forgotten as a dead man out of mind,' while enemies plot. He feels abandoned by all. There are depths of loneliness God permits, that we may lean on Him alone.

He is scorned by neighbours and dreaded by friends, 'forgotten as a dead man out of mind,' while enemies plot. He feels abandoned by all. There are depths of loneliness God permits, that we may lean on Him alone.

He is scorned by neighbours and dreaded by friends, 'forgotten as a dead man out of mind,' while enemies plot. He feels abandoned by all. There are depths of loneliness God permits, that we may lean on Him alone.
Yet, 'I trusted in thee, O LORD: I said, Thou art my God. My times are in thy hand.' He rests his whole life in God's keeping. Whatever the season, the believer's days are held by a faithful hand.
Yet, 'I trusted in thee, O LORD: I said, Thou art my God. My times are in thy hand.' He rests his whole life in God's keeping. Whatever the season, the believer's days are held by a faithful hand.
Yet, 'I trusted in thee, O LORD: I said, Thou art my God. My times are in thy hand.' He rests his whole life in God's keeping. Whatever the season, the believer's days are held by a faithful hand.
He asks not to be ashamed and that lying lips be silenced. He commits the slanderers to God. The truth will outlast every falsehood when God vindicates His own.
He asks not to be ashamed and that lying lips be silenced. He commits the slanderers to God. The truth will outlast every falsehood when God vindicates His own.
'Oh how great is thy goodness... laid up for them that fear thee,' hidden safely in God's presence. A rich store awaits the godly. God keeps His best blessings safe for those who reverence Him.
'Oh how great is thy goodness... laid up for them that fear thee,' hidden safely in God's presence. A rich store awaits the godly. God keeps His best blessings safe for those who reverence Him.

David blesses the LORD for marvellous kindness, confessing he had said 'in my haste, I am cut off,' yet God heard him. Despair spoke too soon. God's mercy answers even the cry we thought would go unheard.

David blesses the LORD for marvellous kindness, confessing he had said 'in my haste, I am cut off,' yet God heard him. Despair spoke too soon. God's mercy answers even the cry we thought would go unheard.

He calls the saints to love the LORD and 'be of good courage... all ye that hope in the LORD.' Faith ends in encouragement. Those who wait on God are bidden to take heart, for He strengthens the hoping soul.

He calls the saints to love the LORD and 'be of good courage... all ye that hope in the LORD.' Faith ends in encouragement. Those who wait on God are bidden to take heart, for He strengthens the hoping soul.
